Untitled Project
GHOST Memory Recording and Files Requirements
Deep Traceability
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • 1. 1. Purpose and Scope
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
      2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
    1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PRODUCT-NAME
      1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary GHOST-MEM-REC-PRODUCT-NAME The product described by this document shall be named GHOST; current Python implementation paths may remain under ragstream.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-MEMORY-PARENT
      1.3. Memory Management Parent GHOST-MEM-REC-MEMORY-PARENT Memory Recording and Files shall be governed by the authority model, capability boundaries, configuration governance, and sibling ownership defined in 50_memory_management.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-BOUNDARY
      1.4. Recording and File Boundary GHOST-MEM-REC-BOUNDARY This document shall own MemoryRecord structure, active-history state, capture actions, .ragmem persistence, .ragmeta.json persistence, SQLite metadata mirroring, history create/load/rename/delete operations, and current ChatGPT shared-link import.

      It shall not own semantic retrieval scoring, runtime compression, final Memory Context synthesis, or document Knowledge Management.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CURRENT-COMPONENTS
      1.5. Current Recording Components GHOST-MEM-REC-CURRENT-COMPONENTS The current capability shall be organized around MemoryRecord, MemoryManager, capture_memory_pair, MemoryFileManager, the ChatGPT shared-link importer, Streamlit file actions, and session initialization.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-DURABLE-FIRST
      1.6. Durable Recording Priority GHOST-MEM-REC-DURABLE-FIRST Durable MemoryRecord creation shall complete before optional derivative vector ingestion is scheduled.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-RECORD
      2.1. MemoryRecord Path GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-RECORD MemoryRecord implementation shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/memory_record.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-MANAGER
      2.2. MemoryManager Path GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-MANAGER Active history, persistence, and SQLite synchronization shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/memory_manager.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-ACTIONS
      2.3. Capture Action Path GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-ACTIONS Reusable pair-capture workflow shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/memory_actions.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-FILE-MANAGER
      2.4. File Manager Path GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-FILE-MANAGER History-level create, load, rename, and delete operations shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/storage/memory_file_manager.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-IMPORTERS
      2.5. Importer Paths GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-IMPORTERS Current ChatGPT import behavior shall be traceable to ragstream/memory/importers/chatgpt_shared_link_importer.py and chatgpt_shared_link_importer_helpers.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-UI
      2.6. UI Wiring Paths GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-UI Current manual-feed and file-action wiring shall be traceable to ragstream/app/ui_actions.py, ragstream/app/ui_actions_files.py, and ragstream/app/ui_streamlit.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-DATA-ROOT
      2.7. Memory Data Root GHOST-MEM-REC-DATA-ROOT Current runtime memory data shall be rooted at data/memory/.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILES-ROOT
        2.8. Memory Files Directory GHOST-MEM-REC-FILES-ROOT Memory history body and metadata files shall be stored under data/memory/files/.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-PATH
        2.9. Memory SQLite Path GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-PATH The current metadata index shall be stored at data/memory/memory_index.sqlite3.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-VECTOR-BOUNDARY
        2.10. Vector Directory Boundary GHOST-MEM-REC-VECTOR-BOUNDARY data/memory/vector_db/ shall be owned by Memory Ingestion and Retrieval requirements rather than by durable file truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ID
      3.1. Record Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-ID Each new MemoryRecord shall receive a record_id; the current generated form shall be a UUID hexadecimal string when no record_id is supplied.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-PARENT-ID
        3.2. Parent Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-PARENT-ID MemoryRecord shall support an optional parent_id without using parent_id as the record identity.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CREATED-UTC
        3.3. Creation Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-CREATED-UTC MemoryRecord shall store created_at_utc in UTC ISO-8601 form with a Z suffix when generated by the current implementation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT
      3.4. Input Text GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT MemoryRecord shall preserve the accepted input_text as stable body content.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT-HASH
        3.7. Input Hash GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT-HASH MemoryRecord shall store SHA-256 of input_text unless a persisted input_hash is supplied during loading.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT
      3.5. Output Text GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT MemoryRecord shall preserve the accepted output_text as stable body content.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT-HASH
        3.8. Output Hash GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT-HASH MemoryRecord shall store SHA-256 of output_text unless a persisted output_hash is supplied during loading.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE
      3.6. Source Field GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE MemoryRecord shall preserve a source string identifying the capture or import origin.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG
      3.9. Record Tag GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG MemoryRecord shall carry a current tag whose capture default is Green.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG-CATALOG
        3.10. Current Tag Catalog GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG-CATALOG MemoryManager shall expose the current tag catalog Gold, Green, and Black.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-USER-KEYWORDS
      3.11. User Keywords GHOST-MEM-REC-USER-KEYWORDS MemoryRecord shall store an ordered case-insensitively deduplicated list of non-empty user_keywords.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS
      3.12. Automatic Keywords GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS MemoryRecord shall store automatic keywords generated from combined input/output text or loaded from persisted data.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE
        4.1. Current Automatic Keyword Generator GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE The current automatic keyword generator shall attempt YAKE extraction over input_text and output_text combined.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-BASELINE
          4.2. Current YAKE Baseline GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-BASELINE The current YAKE call shall use English, maximum n-gram length 3, deduplication limit 0.9, and top 5 results.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-UNAVAILABLE
          4.3. YAKE Unavailable Behavior GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-UNAVAILABLE If YAKE cannot be imported or keyword extraction fails, automatic keyword generation shall return an empty list without preventing MemoryRecord creation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PROJECT
      3.13. Active Project Snapshot Name GHOST-MEM-REC-PROJECT MemoryRecord shall support active_project_name as current metadata describing the project active during capture or import.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-SNAPSHOT
      3.14. Embedded File Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-SNAPSHOT MemoryRecord shall support embedded_files_snapshot as an ordered list captured with the interaction.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE-MODE
      3.15. Retrieval Source Mode GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE-MODE MemoryRecord shall support retrieval_source_mode values QA, Q, or A; invalid or absent values shall normalize to QA.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-DIRECT-KEY
      3.16. Direct Recall Key GHOST-MEM-REC-DIRECT-KEY MemoryRecord shall support a trimmed direct_recall_key string whose capture default is empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE
      3.17. ActiveBrief Title Field GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE MemoryRecord shall support active_retrieval_brief_title as stable body content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-BODY
      3.18. ActiveBrief Body Field GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-BODY MemoryRecord shall support active_retrieval_brief as stable body content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTORS
      3.19. ActiveBrief Contributor Identifiers GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTORS MemoryRecord shall support an ordered deduplicated active_retrieval_brief_contributor_ids list as stable body content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LIST-NORMALIZATION
      3.20. List Normalization GHOST-MEM-REC-LIST-NORMALIZATION MemoryRecord list normalization shall trim values, remove empty values, and remove case-insensitive duplicates while preserving first occurrence order.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-EDITABLE-FIELDS
      4.4. Editable Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-EDITABLE-FIELDS Current editable per-record metadata shall include tag, user_keywords, retrieval_source_mode, and direct_recall_key.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-METADATA-UPDATE
        4.5. Editable Metadata Update GHOST-MEM-REC-METADATA-UPDATE Updating editable metadata shall not change record_id, stable Q/A text, hashes, source, creation timestamp, or persisted ActiveBrief body fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-TAG-VALIDATION
        4.6. GUI Tag Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-TAG-VALIDATION MemoryManager.sync_gui_edits shall accept a tag update only when the value belongs to the current tag catalog.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-KEYWORDS-TYPE
        4.7. GUI Keyword Type Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-KEYWORDS-TYPE A non-list user_keywords value supplied to MemoryManager.sync_gui_edits shall normalize to an empty list.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-SOURCE-MODE
        4.8. GUI Source Mode Normalization GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-SOURCE-MODE GUI retrieval_source_mode shall normalize to QA, Q, or A, with invalid non-null values becoming QA.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-DIRECT-KEY
        4.9. GUI Direct Key Normalization GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-DIRECT-KEY GUI direct_recall_key shall be converted to a trimmed string when supplied.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-CHANGE-DETECTION
        4.10. Metadata Change Detection GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-CHANGE-DETECTION MemoryManager shall compare before and after index views and shall rewrite metadata and refresh SQLite only when at least one matched record changes.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-AUTO-REINGEST-EDIT
        4.11. No Current Automatic Vector Re-Ingestion after Edit GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-AUTO-REINGEST-EDIT Changing editable metadata through sync_gui_edits shall not currently schedule memory-vector re-ingestion by itself.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-MANAGER-INIT
      5.1. MemoryManager Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-MANAGER-INIT MemoryManager initialization shall create memory_root and files_root directories and initialize the SQLite schema.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-NO-FILE
        5.2. No Automatic History File without Title GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-NO-FILE Constructing MemoryManager with an empty title shall not create a .ragmem or .ragmeta.json history file.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-ID
        5.3. Initial Manager Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-ID MemoryManager shall hold a generated file_id even before an active history file is created.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-START-TITLE
        5.4. New History Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-START-TITLE start_new_history shall reject an empty title.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-START-RESET
          5.5. New History State Reset GHOST-MEM-REC-START-RESET start_new_history shall generate a new file_id and reset records, metainfo, pending_activebrief_topic_buffer, and b_file_created.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-PATTERN
          5.6. History Filename Pattern GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-PATTERN The current new-history filename stem shall combine local date/time to minute precision with a sanitized title.

          The paired filenames shall end in .ragmem and .ragmeta.json.
          • GHOST-MEM-REC-SAFE-TITLE
            5.7. Filename Title Sanitization GHOST-MEM-REC-SAFE-TITLE Filename title sanitation shall replace non-alphanumeric/non-dot/non-underscore/non-hyphen sequences with hyphens, collapse repeated hyphens, strip boundary punctuation, and use Untitled when empty.
          • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-COLLISION
            5.8. Filename Collision Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-COLLISION When the generated .ragmem filename already exists, the stem shall append the first eight characters of file_id before assigning paired filenames.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION
      5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-INPUT
        5.10. Capture Input Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-INPUT capture_memory_pair shall return unsuccessful status and create no record when input_text is empty after trimming.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-OUTPUT
        5.11. Capture Output Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-OUTPUT capture_memory_pair shall return unsuccessful status and create no record when output_text is empty after trimming.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-SYNC-FIRST
        5.12. Synchronize Existing Edits Before Capture GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-SYNC-FIRST capture_memory_pair shall synchronize supplied GUI record edits before creating the new MemoryRecord.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-DEFAULTS
        5.13. Captured Record Defaults GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-DEFAULTS MemoryManager.capture_pair shall create the new record with tag Green, retrieval_source_mode QA, and empty direct_recall_key.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-HISTORY
        5.14. Automatic History on First Capture GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-HISTORY If no active title exists, the first capture shall start a history whose current automatic title is memory-record.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-BEFORE-APPEND
        5.15. ActiveBrief Before Durable Append GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-BEFORE-APPEND MemoryManager shall attempt ActiveRetrievalBrief creation before the new record is appended to .ragmem so the resulting brief fields can be included in the stable body.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-PERSIST-SEQUENCE
        5.16. Capture Persistence Sequence GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-PERSIST-SEQUENCE After ActiveBrief processing, MemoryManager shall append the record to its in-memory list, append one record block to .ragmem, rewrite .ragmeta.json, and refresh SQLite.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CAPTURE
          14.3. AC: Durable Pair Capture GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CAPTURE Given non-empty input and output, capture shall produce one MemoryRecord, append one stable .ragmem block, update .ragmeta.json, and mirror the record in SQLite.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-RETURN
        5.17. Capture Result GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-RETURN A successful capture action shall return success, the MemoryRecord, record_id, file_id, and active .ragmem filename.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE
        5.18. Optional Ingestion Scheduling GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE When a memory_ingestion_manager is supplied, capture_memory_pair shall attempt to schedule asynchronous ingestion after durable capture completes.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL
          5.19. Ingestion Scheduling Failure GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL If vector-ingestion scheduling raises an exception, the action shall retain the saved MemoryRecord and report a warning rather than undo durable capture.
          • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL
            14.12. AC: Vector Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL If asynchronous vector ingestion cannot be scheduled after capture, the .ragmem body, metadata, and SQLite row shall remain saved.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY
      6.1. .ragmem Stable Body Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY The .ragmem file shall remain the authority for stable MemoryRecord body fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MARKERS
        6.2. Record Block Markers GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MARKERS Each .ragmem record block shall be delimited by the exact current start and end markers defined by MemoryRecord.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-JSON
        6.3. Record Block JSON GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-JSON The content between record markers shall be one UTF-8 JSON object serialized with ensure_ascii disabled and indentation.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-FIELDS
        6.4. Stable Body Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-FIELDS The current .ragmem body shall contain record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, input_text, output_text, source, input_hash, output_hash, active_retrieval_brief_title, active_retrieval_brief, and active_retrieval_brief_contributor_ids.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-EDITABLE
        6.5. Editable Metadata Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-EDITABLE Current tag, retrieval_source_mode, direct_recall_key, automatic keywords, user keywords, active project name, and embedded-file snapshot shall not be duplicated into newly written body-only .ragmem blocks.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-APPEND
        6.6. Record Append Behavior GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-APPEND Normal pair capture shall open the active .ragmem file in append mode, write one complete MemoryRecord block, and preserve existing blocks.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-CREATED-FLAG
          6.7. File-Created Flag GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-CREATED-FLAG After a record block is appended, MemoryManager shall mark b_file_created true.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ
        6.8. Record Block Loading GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ Loading a history shall locate marker-delimited blocks, parse JSON objects, and construct MemoryRecords in file order.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MALFORMED-BLOCK
          6.9. Malformed Block Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MALFORMED-BLOCK A marker-delimited block that cannot be parsed into a JSON object shall be skipped by the current loader without preventing other valid blocks from loading.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-BACKWARD
          6.10. Older Full-Block Compatibility GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-BACKWARD MemoryRecord.from_dict shall accept older .ragmem blocks that contain metadata in addition to stable body fields; current .ragmeta.json metadata shall be overlaid later when available.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-VECTORS
        6.11. No Vector Data in .ragmem GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-VECTORS The .ragmem body shall not store embedding vectors, similarity scores, runtime candidates, compression output, or synthesized Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
      7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-REBUILD
        7.2. Metadata Rebuild from Records GHOST-MEM-REC-META-REBUILD MemoryManager.save_metainfo shall rebuild metainfo from the current in-memory record list before writing the file.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS
        7.3. File-Level Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS The current metadata file shall include file_id, title, paired filenames, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, record_count, record_ids, parent_ids, tag_summary, aggregate automatic keywords, aggregate user keywords, and per-record metadata views.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-CREATED
          7.4. Metadata Created Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-META-CREATED For a non-empty history, created_at_utc shall be taken from the first MemoryRecord; for an empty in-memory history built by MemoryManager it may be empty.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-UPDATED
          7.5. Metadata Updated Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-META-UPDATED For a non-empty history, updated_at_utc shall be refreshed to current UTC when metainfo is rebuilt.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AGGREGATES
          7.6. Metadata Aggregate Deduplication GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AGGREGATES Aggregate parent identifiers and keyword lists shall be deduplicated while preserving first occurrence order.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-RECORD-VIEW
        7.7. Per-Record Metadata View GHOST-MEM-REC-META-RECORD-VIEW Each per-record metadata entry shall use MemoryRecord.to_index_dict and shall not duplicate full input/output or full ActiveBrief text.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-OVERLAY
        7.8. Metadata Overlay on Load GHOST-MEM-REC-META-OVERLAY When loading a history with an existing metadata file, MemoryManager shall apply metadata entries to matching record_id values after loading stable bodies from .ragmem.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-STABLE-OVERWRITE
          7.9. Stable Field Overlay Protection GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-STABLE-OVERWRITE Metadata overlay shall not modify record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, input/output text, source, hashes, or stable ActiveBrief fields.
          • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-AUTHORITY
            14.5. AC: Body and Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-AUTHORITY Editing a tag shall change .ragmeta.json and SQLite metadata while leaving the corresponding .ragmem stable body unchanged.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MISSING
        7.10. Missing Metadata File GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MISSING When a history is loaded and its .ragmeta.json file is missing, MemoryManager shall rebuild and save metainfo from loaded records.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MALFORMED
        7.11. Malformed Metadata File GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MALFORMED Malformed .ragmeta.json input shall not be silently interpreted as valid metadata by MemoryManager.load_history; the JSON loading failure shall remain visible to the caller.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-RUNTIME
        7.12. Runtime Artifact Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-RUNTIME The metadata file shall not store raw vector hits, MemoryContextPack, compression results, synthesis diagnostics, or synthesized Memory Context.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-NO-RUNTIME-PERSIST
          14.13. AC: Runtime Artifact Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-NO-RUNTIME-PERSIST MemoryContextPack, query-dependent compression, and synthesized Memory Context shall not appear in newly written .ragmem or .ragmeta.json data.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-PK
        8.2. Memory File Primary Key GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-PK memory_files.file_id shall be the table primary key.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-PK
        8.3. Memory Record Composite Key GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-PK memory_records shall use the composite primary key file_id and record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-COLUMNS
        8.4. File Table Columns GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-COLUMNS memory_files shall store file_id, title, filename_ragmem, filename_meta, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, and record_count.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-COLUMNS
        8.5. Record Table Columns GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-COLUMNS memory_records shall store file_id, record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, source, tag, retrieval_source_mode, direct_recall_key, keyword JSON, project/file snapshot JSON, and input/output hashes.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-NO-BODY
        8.6. No Full Q/A in SQLite GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-NO-BODY memory_records shall not store full input_text, output_text, or full ActiveRetrievalBrief text.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-FILE
        8.7. File Row Upsert GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-FILE refresh_sqlite_index shall insert or update the active memory_files row by file_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-RECORD
        8.8. Record Row Upsert GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-RECORD refresh_sqlite_index shall insert or update every current MemoryRecord row by file_id and record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIRROR-DELETE
        8.9. Mirror Row Cleanup GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIRROR-DELETE After upserting the active record list, SQLite rows for the active file_id whose record_id is no longer present shall be deleted.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-EMPTY-CLEAN
          8.10. Empty Active Record Cleanup GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-EMPTY-CLEAN When the active MemoryManager record list is empty, refresh shall delete all memory_records rows for the active file_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-TAG
        8.11. Tag Index GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-TAG The current schema shall create an index on memory_records.tag.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-PROJECT
        8.12. Project Index GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-PROJECT The current schema shall create an index on memory_records.active_project_name.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-KEY
        8.13. Direct Recall Key Index GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-KEY The current schema shall create an index on memory_records.direct_recall_key.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIGRATION
        8.14. Current Column Migration GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIGRATION Schema initialization shall add retrieval_source_mode and direct_recall_key columns with current defaults when loading an older memory_records table that lacks them.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-LIST
        8.15. History Listing Order GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-LIST MemoryManager.list_histories shall return memory_files rows ordered by updated_at_utc descending.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-ID
      9.1. History Lookup by file_id GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-ID MemoryManager.load_history shall resolve the requested history through memory_files.file_id and shall fail when no matching row exists.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-FIELDS
        9.2. Loaded File-Level State GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-FIELDS Loading shall set file_id, title, filename_ragmem, and filename_meta from the SQLite file row.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-RECORDS
        9.3. Loaded Record State GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-RECORDS Loading shall replace the current record list with MemoryRecords read from the selected .ragmem file.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-PENDING-RESET
        9.4. Pending Buffer Reset on Load GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-PENDING-RESET Loading a history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to empty.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-CREATED-FLAG
        9.5. Loaded File-Created Flag GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-CREATED-FLAG After load, b_file_created shall indicate whether the selected .ragmem path exists.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-REFRESH
        9.6. SQLite Refresh after Load GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-REFRESH After loading bodies and metadata, MemoryManager shall refresh the SQLite mirror for the active history.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-LOAD
          14.6. AC: History Load GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-LOAD Given a valid indexed history, loading shall restore record order and stable bodies, overlay current metadata, reset pending-topic state, and refresh SQLite.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CLOSE
      9.7. MemoryManager Close GHOST-MEM-REC-CLOSE MemoryManager.close shall save current metainfo and refresh the SQLite index.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-LIST
      10.1. List Histories GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-LIST MemoryFileManager.list_histories shall return the MemoryManager SQLite history listing.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE
      10.2. Create History Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE MemoryFileManager.create_history shall reject an empty title.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE
        10.3. Create Empty History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE Creating an empty history shall make it active, create an empty .ragmem file, create paired .ragmeta.json content, and create or update the memory_files row with record_count zero.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TIMES
          10.4. Empty History Timestamps GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TIMES An explicitly created empty history shall use current UTC for both created_at_utc and updated_at_utc in its initial metadata.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CREATE
          14.7. AC: Empty History Creation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CREATE Creating a named empty history shall produce paired empty/body metadata files and a file-level SQLite row with zero records.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-LOAD
      10.5. File Manager Load GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-LOAD MemoryFileManager.load_history shall validate file_id and delegate active-history loading to MemoryManager.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE
      10.6. Rename Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE MemoryFileManager.rename_history shall reject an empty file_id or new title and shall reject unknown file_id values.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ID
        10.7. Rename Preserves file_id GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ID Renaming a memory history shall preserve file_id.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-RENAME
          14.8. AC: Stable Rename Identity GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-RENAME Renaming a history shall preserve file_id while updating paired filenames, title, metadata file fields, and the SQLite file row.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-PAIR
        10.8. Rename Paired Files GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-PAIR Renaming shall rename the physical .ragmem and .ragmeta.json files when they exist and update their stored filename fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TIMESTAMP
        10.9. Rename Timestamp Prefix GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TIMESTAMP Renaming shall preserve an existing YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM filename prefix when present; otherwise it shall use the current minute prefix.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-COLLISION
        10.10. Rename Collision Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-COLLISION When either target filename already exists, rename shall append the first eight characters of file_id to the new stem.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-SQLITE
        10.11. Rename SQLite Update GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-SQLITE Renaming shall update title, paired filenames, and updated_at_utc in memory_files.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ACTIVE
        10.12. Rename Active History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ACTIVE When the renamed history is active, MemoryManager file-level state shall be updated and its metadata and SQLite mirror shall be refreshed.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-INACTIVE
        10.13. Rename Inactive History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-INACTIVE When the renamed history is not active, the renamed metadata file shall have its file-level title, paired filenames, and updated timestamp updated without loading the history.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE
      10.14. Delete History Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE MemoryFileManager.delete_history shall reject an empty or unknown file_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VECTORS
        10.15. Delete History Vectors GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VECTORS When a compatible MemoryVectorStore is supplied, deleting a history shall request deletion of vectors by file_id before deleting SQLite rows and files.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-SQLITE
        10.16. Delete History SQLite Rows GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-SQLITE Deleting a history shall remove matching rows from memory_records and memory_files.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-PHYSICAL
        10.17. Delete History Physical Files GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-PHYSICAL Deleting a history shall unlink its .ragmem and .ragmeta.json files when present.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-DELETE
          14.9. AC: Complete History Delete GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-DELETE Deleting a selected history shall remove its SQLite rows, physical paired files, and memory vectors when the vector store is connected.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-ACTIVE
        10.18. Delete Active History State GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-ACTIVE When the deleted history is active, the current implementation shall replace file_id, clear title and filenames, clear records and metainfo, and mark b_file_created false.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-REPORT
        10.19. Delete History Report GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-REPORT The delete result shall report file identity, prior filenames, title, and number of vectors deleted when available.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-DELETE-CONFIRM
        10.20. Typed Delete Confirmation GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-DELETE-CONFIRM The current Streamlit file action shall require the exact trimmed text delete before confirming history deletion.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-URL
      11.1. Shared URL Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-URL The current importer shall accept only a non-empty URL matching https://chatgpt.com/share/<conversation-id> with the supported identifier characters and optional query string.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-TITLE
      11.2. Import Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-TITLE The current importer shall require a non-empty title.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PLAYWRIGHT
      11.3. Playwright Dependency GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PLAYWRIGHT Current shared-page extraction shall use Playwright Chromium and shall report an explicit dependency error when Playwright is unavailable.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-NAVIGATION
      11.4. Shared Page Navigation GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-NAVIGATION The importer shall navigate with domcontentloaded, bounded timeout, and current retry handling for specified transient network errors.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-EXTRACTION-ORDER
      11.5. Message Extraction Sources GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-EXTRACTION-ORDER The importer shall attempt visible DOM message extraction and embedded structured-message extraction and shall retain body text as fallback input.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-STABILITY
      11.6. Message Stability Wait GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-STABILITY When enabled, current extraction shall scroll and wait for visible message count stability before selecting the message source.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-ROLES
      11.7. Supported Imported Roles GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-ROLES Only user and assistant messages shall participate in Q/A pair creation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PAIRING
      11.8. Imported Message Pairing GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PAIRING Ordered user content followed by assistant content shall be combined into one input/output pair; a later user message shall flush the completed prior pair.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RAW-FALLBACK
      11.9. Raw Text Pair Fallback GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RAW-FALLBACK When no structured Q/A pair is produced but cleaned body text exists, the importer shall create one pair whose input identifies the imported conversation and whose output is the body text.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT-FALLBACK
        14.11. AC: Import Body Fallback GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT-FALLBACK Given no structured Q/A pairs but usable body text, import shall create exactly one fallback pair rather than silently report a structured extraction success.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-NO-CONTENT
      11.10. No Importable Content GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-NO-CONTENT When neither pairs nor usable fallback text exist, the import shall fail explicitly.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RECORDS
      11.11. Imported MemoryRecord Construction GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RECORDS Each imported pair shall become a normal MemoryRecord with source prefixed chatgpt_shared_link:, tag Green, retrieval source mode QA, empty Direct Recall Key, and user keyword chatgpt_shared_link.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PARENT-CHAIN
        11.12. Imported Parent Chain GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PARENT-CHAIN Imported records shall set each record parent_id to the immediately preceding imported record_id, with the first parent_id empty.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT
        11.13. Imported Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT The importer shall apply the supplied active project name and embedded-file snapshot to each imported record.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-BRIEF
      11.14. Optional Shared Memory Brief GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-BRIEF When a non-empty import memory_brief is supplied, the importer shall assign that brief and its title to every imported record and shall use all imported record IDs as contributor IDs.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST
      11.15. Imported History Persistence GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST Imported records shall be persisted as a new ordinary memory history by writing all record blocks to a new .ragmem file, saving metadata, and refreshing SQLite.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PENDING-RESET
        11.16. Imported Pending Buffer Reset GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PENDING-RESET Persisting an imported history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to empty.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-INGEST
        11.17. Optional Imported Vector Ingestion GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-INGEST When MemoryIngestionManager is available, the importer shall call ingest_all after durable history persistence.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT
        14.10. AC: Shared Conversation Import GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT Given a valid public shared conversation with importable user/assistant content, import shall create a new ordinary memory history whose Q/A pairs are normal MemoryRecords and may be vector-ingested after persistence.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-REPORT
      11.18. Import Result Report GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-REPORT A successful import shall report URL, title, file identity, paired filenames, record/pair/message counts, raw text character count, extraction method, brief assignment status, and optional vector-ingestion result.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-MANAGER
      12.1. Session MemoryManager Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-MANAGER Current Streamlit startup shall create one MemoryManager in session state with data/memory as memory_root and memory_index.sqlite3 as sqlite_path.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-CHUNKER
      12.2. Session MemoryChunker Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-CHUNKER Current Streamlit startup shall create one MemoryChunker in session state when absent.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-STORE
      12.3. Session MemoryVectorStore Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-STORE Current Streamlit startup shall create one MemoryVectorStore in session state under data/memory/vector_db with collection memory_vectors.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-INGEST
      12.4. Session MemoryIngestionManager Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-INGEST Current Streamlit startup shall construct MemoryIngestionManager from the session MemoryManager, MemoryChunker, and MemoryVectorStore.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-MANUAL-FEED
      12.5. Manual Memory Feed GHOST-MEM-REC-MANUAL-FEED The current manual memory-feed action shall read prompt_text and manual_memory_feed_text, reject either empty value, capture active project/file snapshot, collect editable record metadata, and call capture_memory_pair with source manual_memory_feed.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILES-ACTIONS
      12.6. Files Page Actions GHOST-MEM-REC-FILES-ACTIONS The current files action layer shall delegate create, load, rename, delete, and import operations to MemoryFileManager or the ChatGPT importer rather than implement persistence directly.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-WIDGET-CLEAR
      12.7. Memory Widget State Reset GHOST-MEM-REC-WIDGET-CLEAR After file create, load, delete, or import, the current UI shall clear per-record tag, retrieval-mode, user-keyword, and Direct Recall widget state keys.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVE-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT
      12.8. UI Active Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVE-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT When an active project exists, the current UI shall attempt to capture the project name and controller-reported embedded file list for new or imported records.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-NO-PROJECT
        12.9. No Active Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-NO-PROJECT When no active project exists, current capture/import wiring shall use no project name and an empty embedded-file snapshot.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-EMPTY-PAIR
      13.1. No Empty Memory Pair GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-EMPTY-PAIR The recording action shall not create a MemoryRecord from an empty prompt or empty response.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-EMPTY
        14.4. AC: Empty Pair Rejection GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-EMPTY Given empty input or output after trimming, no record, file append, metadata rewrite, SQLite row, or vector-ingestion schedule shall be produced.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-ID-VALIDATION
      13.2. File Identifier Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-ID-VALIDATION File-level operations shall reject empty file_id values rather than execute broad file or database operations.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-SANITATION
      13.3. Filename Path Sanitization GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-SANITATION User-provided history titles shall not be used directly as path fragments; the current safe-title transformation shall be applied.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE-PRESERVATION
      13.4. Source Preservation GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE-PRESERVATION Memory recording and import shall preserve source metadata sufficient to distinguish manual capture from imported shared conversations.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-HASH-INTEGRITY
      13.5. Body Hash Integrity GHOST-MEM-REC-HASH-INTEGRITY New MemoryRecords shall compute input/output hashes from exact stored text and shall preserve loaded persisted hashes when present.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-SECRET
      13.6. No Secret Persistence GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-SECRET Memory body and metadata persistence shall not intentionally store provider API keys or browser credentials.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PUBLIC-LOG
      13.7. Public Recording Logs GHOST-MEM-REC-PUBLIC-LOG Public recording logs shall use concise file or record identifiers and status messages and shall not emit full MemoryRecord bodies by default.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CONFIDENTIAL-LOG
      13.8. Confidential Developer Logs GHOST-MEM-REC-CONFIDENTIAL-LOG Complete memory payloads or detailed importer diagnostics shall remain behind explicitly enabled confidential developer logging.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-ORDER
      13.9. Delete Ordering GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-ORDER Current history deletion shall attempt vector cleanup, then delete SQLite rows, then unlink physical files, preserving the actual execution order for diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-VECTOR-TRUTH
      13.10. No Vector Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-VECTOR-TRUTH Successful or failed vector ingestion shall not change which stable input/output body is authoritative.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-TESTABILITY
      13.11. Recording Testability GHOST-MEM-REC-TESTABILITY MemoryRecord serialization, title sanitation, metadata rebuild, SQLite mirroring, load overlay, and file operations shall be testable without provider LLM calls.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CODE-OWNERSHIP
      14.1. Recording Code Ownership GHOST-MEM-REC-CODE-OWNERSHIP MemoryRecord, MemoryManager, memory actions, file manager, import helpers, and file UI actions shall map to the deepest applicable UIDs in this document.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-SYNC
        14.14. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-SYNC A recording baseline shall not be accepted when stable-body fields, metadata authority, file identity, capture order, SQLite mirror semantics, or history operations contradict this document.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SIBLING-BOUNDARY
      14.2. Sibling Ownership Boundary GHOST-MEM-REC-SIBLING-BOUNDARY Memory vector ingestion and retrieval shall remain owned by 52; ActiveBrief algorithms, runtime compression, and synthesis shall remain owned by 53.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-PRODUCT-NAME
    1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary GHOST-MEM-REC-PRODUCT-NAME The product described by this document shall be named GHOST; current Python implementation paths may remain under ragstream.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-MEMORY-PARENT
    1.3. Memory Management Parent GHOST-MEM-REC-MEMORY-PARENT Memory Recording and Files shall be governed by the authority model, capability boundaries, configuration governance, and sibling ownership defined in 50_memory_management.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-BOUNDARY
    1.4. Recording and File Boundary GHOST-MEM-REC-BOUNDARY This document shall own MemoryRecord structure, active-history state, capture actions, .ragmem persistence, .ragmeta.json persistence, SQLite metadata mirroring, history create/load/rename/delete operations, and current ChatGPT shared-link import.

    It shall not own semantic retrieval scoring, runtime compression, final Memory Context synthesis, or document Knowledge Management.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-CURRENT-COMPONENTS
    1.5. Current Recording Components GHOST-MEM-REC-CURRENT-COMPONENTS The current capability shall be organized around MemoryRecord, MemoryManager, capture_memory_pair, MemoryFileManager, the ChatGPT shared-link importer, Streamlit file actions, and session initialization.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-DURABLE-FIRST
    1.6. Durable Recording Priority GHOST-MEM-REC-DURABLE-FIRST Durable MemoryRecord creation shall complete before optional derivative vector ingestion is scheduled.

      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-UI
    2.6. UI Wiring Paths GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-UI Current manual-feed and file-action wiring shall be traceable to ragstream/app/ui_actions.py, ragstream/app/ui_actions_files.py, and ragstream/app/ui_streamlit.py.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-DATA-ROOT
    2.7. Memory Data Root GHOST-MEM-REC-DATA-ROOT Current runtime memory data shall be rooted at data/memory/.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILES-ROOT
      2.8. Memory Files Directory GHOST-MEM-REC-FILES-ROOT Memory history body and metadata files shall be stored under data/memory/files/.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-PATH
      2.9. Memory SQLite Path GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-PATH The current metadata index shall be stored at data/memory/memory_index.sqlite3.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-VECTOR-BOUNDARY
      2.10. Vector Directory Boundary GHOST-MEM-REC-VECTOR-BOUNDARY data/memory/vector_db/ shall be owned by Memory Ingestion and Retrieval requirements rather than by durable file truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-DATA-ROOT
      2.7. Memory Data Root GHOST-MEM-REC-DATA-ROOT Current runtime memory data shall be rooted at data/memory/.

    3. 3. MemoryRecord Identity and Field Model
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-ID
    3.1. Record Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-ID Each new MemoryRecord shall receive a record_id; the current generated form shall be a UUID hexadecimal string when no record_id is supplied.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-PARENT-ID
      3.2. Parent Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-PARENT-ID MemoryRecord shall support an optional parent_id without using parent_id as the record identity.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CREATED-UTC
      3.3. Creation Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-CREATED-UTC MemoryRecord shall store created_at_utc in UTC ISO-8601 form with a Z suffix when generated by the current implementation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ID
      3.1. Record Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-ID Each new MemoryRecord shall receive a record_id; the current generated form shall be a UUID hexadecimal string when no record_id is supplied.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-PARENT-ID
    3.2. Parent Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-PARENT-ID MemoryRecord shall support an optional parent_id without using parent_id as the record identity.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ID
      3.1. Record Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-ID Each new MemoryRecord shall receive a record_id; the current generated form shall be a UUID hexadecimal string when no record_id is supplied.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree.     GHOST-MEM-REC-CREATED-UTC
    3.3. Creation Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-CREATED-UTC MemoryRecord shall store created_at_utc in UTC ISO-8601 form with a Z suffix when generated by the current implementation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT
    3.4. Input Text GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT MemoryRecord shall preserve the accepted input_text as stable body content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT-HASH
      3.7. Input Hash GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT-HASH MemoryRecord shall store SHA-256 of input_text unless a persisted input_hash is supplied during loading.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT
    3.5. Output Text GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT MemoryRecord shall preserve the accepted output_text as stable body content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT-HASH
      3.8. Output Hash GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT-HASH MemoryRecord shall store SHA-256 of output_text unless a persisted output_hash is supplied during loading.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE
    3.6. Source Field GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE MemoryRecord shall preserve a source string identifying the capture or import origin.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT
      3.4. Input Text GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT MemoryRecord shall preserve the accepted input_text as stable body content.

        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    3.7. Input Hash GHOST-MEM-REC-INPUT-HASH MemoryRecord shall store SHA-256 of input_text unless a persisted input_hash is supplied during loading.

      3.5. Output Text GHOST-MEM-REC-OUTPUT MemoryRecord shall preserve the accepted output_text as stable body content.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG
    3.9. Record Tag GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG MemoryRecord shall carry a current tag whose capture default is Green.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG-CATALOG
      3.10. Current Tag Catalog GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG-CATALOG MemoryManager shall expose the current tag catalog Gold, Green, and Black.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG
      3.9. Record Tag GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG MemoryRecord shall carry a current tag whose capture default is Green.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG-CATALOG
    3.10. Current Tag Catalog GHOST-MEM-REC-TAG-CATALOG MemoryManager shall expose the current tag catalog Gold, Green, and Black.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-USER-KEYWORDS
    3.11. User Keywords GHOST-MEM-REC-USER-KEYWORDS MemoryRecord shall store an ordered case-insensitively deduplicated list of non-empty user_keywords.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS
    3.12. Automatic Keywords GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS MemoryRecord shall store automatic keywords generated from combined input/output text or loaded from persisted data.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE
      4.1. Current Automatic Keyword Generator GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE The current automatic keyword generator shall attempt YAKE extraction over input_text and output_text combined.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-BASELINE
        4.2. Current YAKE Baseline GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-BASELINE The current YAKE call shall use English, maximum n-gram length 3, deduplication limit 0.9, and top 5 results.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-UNAVAILABLE
        4.3. YAKE Unavailable Behavior GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-UNAVAILABLE If YAKE cannot be imported or keyword extraction fails, automatic keyword generation shall return an empty list without preventing MemoryRecord creation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-PROJECT
    3.13. Active Project Snapshot Name GHOST-MEM-REC-PROJECT MemoryRecord shall support active_project_name as current metadata describing the project active during capture or import.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-SNAPSHOT
    3.14. Embedded File Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-SNAPSHOT MemoryRecord shall support embedded_files_snapshot as an ordered list captured with the interaction.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE-MODE
    3.15. Retrieval Source Mode GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE-MODE MemoryRecord shall support retrieval_source_mode values QA, Q, or A; invalid or absent values shall normalize to QA.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-DIRECT-KEY
    3.16. Direct Recall Key GHOST-MEM-REC-DIRECT-KEY MemoryRecord shall support a trimmed direct_recall_key string whose capture default is empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree.     GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE
    3.17. ActiveBrief Title Field GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE MemoryRecord shall support active_retrieval_brief_title as stable body content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-BODY
    3.18. ActiveBrief Body Field GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-BODY MemoryRecord shall support active_retrieval_brief as stable body content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTORS
    3.19. ActiveBrief Contributor Identifiers GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTORS MemoryRecord shall support an ordered deduplicated active_retrieval_brief_contributor_ids list as stable body content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-LIST-NORMALIZATION
    3.20. List Normalization GHOST-MEM-REC-LIST-NORMALIZATION MemoryRecord list normalization shall trim values, remove empty values, and remove case-insensitive duplicates while preserving first occurrence order.

    4. 4. Automatic Keywords and Editable Metadata
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS
      3.12. Automatic Keywords GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS MemoryRecord shall store automatic keywords generated from combined input/output text or loaded from persisted data.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE
    4.1. Current Automatic Keyword Generator GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE The current automatic keyword generator shall attempt YAKE extraction over input_text and output_text combined.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-BASELINE
      4.2. Current YAKE Baseline GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-BASELINE The current YAKE call shall use English, maximum n-gram length 3, deduplication limit 0.9, and top 5 results.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-UNAVAILABLE
      4.3. YAKE Unavailable Behavior GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-UNAVAILABLE If YAKE cannot be imported or keyword extraction fails, automatic keyword generation shall return an empty list without preventing MemoryRecord creation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE
      4.1. Current Automatic Keyword Generator GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE The current automatic keyword generator shall attempt YAKE extraction over input_text and output_text combined.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS
        3.12. Automatic Keywords GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS MemoryRecord shall store automatic keywords generated from combined input/output text or loaded from persisted data.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-BASELINE
    4.2. Current YAKE Baseline GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-BASELINE The current YAKE call shall use English, maximum n-gram length 3, deduplication limit 0.9, and top 5 results.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE
      4.1. Current Automatic Keyword Generator GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE The current automatic keyword generator shall attempt YAKE extraction over input_text and output_text combined.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS
        3.12. Automatic Keywords GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-KEYWORDS MemoryRecord shall store automatic keywords generated from combined input/output text or loaded from persisted data.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-UNAVAILABLE
    4.3. YAKE Unavailable Behavior GHOST-MEM-REC-YAKE-UNAVAILABLE If YAKE cannot be imported or keyword extraction fails, automatic keyword generation shall return an empty list without preventing MemoryRecord creation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-EDITABLE-FIELDS
    4.4. Editable Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-EDITABLE-FIELDS Current editable per-record metadata shall include tag, user_keywords, retrieval_source_mode, and direct_recall_key.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-METADATA-UPDATE
      4.5. Editable Metadata Update GHOST-MEM-REC-METADATA-UPDATE Updating editable metadata shall not change record_id, stable Q/A text, hashes, source, creation timestamp, or persisted ActiveBrief body fields.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-TAG-VALIDATION
      4.6. GUI Tag Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-TAG-VALIDATION MemoryManager.sync_gui_edits shall accept a tag update only when the value belongs to the current tag catalog.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-KEYWORDS-TYPE
      4.7. GUI Keyword Type Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-KEYWORDS-TYPE A non-list user_keywords value supplied to MemoryManager.sync_gui_edits shall normalize to an empty list.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-SOURCE-MODE
      4.8. GUI Source Mode Normalization GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-SOURCE-MODE GUI retrieval_source_mode shall normalize to QA, Q, or A, with invalid non-null values becoming QA.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-DIRECT-KEY
      4.9. GUI Direct Key Normalization GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-DIRECT-KEY GUI direct_recall_key shall be converted to a trimmed string when supplied.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-CHANGE-DETECTION
      4.10. Metadata Change Detection GHOST-MEM-REC-GUI-CHANGE-DETECTION MemoryManager shall compare before and after index views and shall rewrite metadata and refresh SQLite only when at least one matched record changes.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-AUTO-REINGEST-EDIT
      4.11. No Current Automatic Vector Re-Ingestion after Edit GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-AUTO-REINGEST-EDIT Changing editable metadata through sync_gui_edits shall not currently schedule memory-vector re-ingestion by itself.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-EDITABLE-FIELDS
      4.4. Editable Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-EDITABLE-FIELDS Current editable per-record metadata shall include tag, user_keywords, retrieval_source_mode, and direct_recall_key.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
