    5. 5. Active History Initialization and Capture Workflow
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-MANAGER-INIT
    5.1. MemoryManager Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-MANAGER-INIT MemoryManager initialization shall create memory_root and files_root directories and initialize the SQLite schema.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-NO-FILE
      5.2. No Automatic History File without Title GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-NO-FILE Constructing MemoryManager with an empty title shall not create a .ragmem or .ragmeta.json history file.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-ID
      5.3. Initial Manager Identifier GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-ID MemoryManager shall hold a generated file_id even before an active history file is created.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-START-TITLE
      5.4. New History Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-START-TITLE start_new_history shall reject an empty title.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-START-RESET
        5.5. New History State Reset GHOST-MEM-REC-START-RESET start_new_history shall generate a new file_id and reset records, metainfo, pending_activebrief_topic_buffer, and b_file_created.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-PATTERN
        5.6. History Filename Pattern GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-PATTERN The current new-history filename stem shall combine local date/time to minute precision with a sanitized title.

        The paired filenames shall end in .ragmem and .ragmeta.json.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-SAFE-TITLE
          5.7. Filename Title Sanitization GHOST-MEM-REC-SAFE-TITLE Filename title sanitation shall replace non-alphanumeric/non-dot/non-underscore/non-hyphen sequences with hyphens, collapse repeated hyphens, strip boundary punctuation, and use Untitled when empty.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-COLLISION
          5.8. Filename Collision Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-COLLISION When the generated .ragmem filename already exists, the stem shall append the first eight characters of file_id before assigning paired filenames.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-MANAGER-INIT
      5.1. MemoryManager Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-MANAGER-INIT MemoryManager initialization shall create memory_root and files_root directories and initialize the SQLite schema.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-NO-FILE
    5.2. No Automatic History File without Title GHOST-MEM-REC-INIT-NO-FILE Constructing MemoryManager with an empty title shall not create a .ragmem or .ragmeta.json history file.

    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-PATTERN
      5.6. History Filename Pattern GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-PATTERN The current new-history filename stem shall combine local date/time to minute precision with a sanitized title.

      The paired filenames shall end in .ragmem and .ragmeta.json.

        5.7. Filename Title Sanitization GHOST-MEM-REC-SAFE-TITLE Filename title sanitation shall replace non-alphanumeric/non-dot/non-underscore/non-hyphen sequences with hyphens, collapse repeated hyphens, strip boundary punctuation, and use Untitled when empty.

          • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
            1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-COLLISION
    5.8. Filename Collision Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-FILENAME-COLLISION When the generated .ragmem filename already exists, the stem shall append the first eight characters of file_id before assigning paired filenames.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION
    5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-INPUT
      5.10. Capture Input Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-INPUT capture_memory_pair shall return unsuccessful status and create no record when input_text is empty after trimming.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-OUTPUT
      5.11. Capture Output Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-OUTPUT capture_memory_pair shall return unsuccessful status and create no record when output_text is empty after trimming.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-SYNC-FIRST
      5.12. Synchronize Existing Edits Before Capture GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-SYNC-FIRST capture_memory_pair shall synchronize supplied GUI record edits before creating the new MemoryRecord.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-DEFAULTS
      5.13. Captured Record Defaults GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-DEFAULTS MemoryManager.capture_pair shall create the new record with tag Green, retrieval_source_mode QA, and empty direct_recall_key.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-HISTORY
      5.14. Automatic History on First Capture GHOST-MEM-REC-AUTO-HISTORY If no active title exists, the first capture shall start a history whose current automatic title is memory-record.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-BEFORE-APPEND
      5.15. ActiveBrief Before Durable Append GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVEBRIEF-BEFORE-APPEND MemoryManager shall attempt ActiveRetrievalBrief creation before the new record is appended to .ragmem so the resulting brief fields can be included in the stable body.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-PERSIST-SEQUENCE
      5.16. Capture Persistence Sequence GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-PERSIST-SEQUENCE After ActiveBrief processing, MemoryManager shall append the record to its in-memory list, append one record block to .ragmem, rewrite .ragmeta.json, and refresh SQLite.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CAPTURE
        14.3. AC: Durable Pair Capture GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CAPTURE Given non-empty input and output, capture shall produce one MemoryRecord, append one stable .ragmem block, update .ragmeta.json, and mirror the record in SQLite.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-RETURN
      5.17. Capture Result GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-RETURN A successful capture action shall return success, the MemoryRecord, record_id, file_id, and active .ragmem filename.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE
      5.18. Optional Ingestion Scheduling GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE When a memory_ingestion_manager is supplied, capture_memory_pair shall attempt to schedule asynchronous ingestion after durable capture completes.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL
        5.19. Ingestion Scheduling Failure GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL If vector-ingestion scheduling raises an exception, the action shall retain the saved MemoryRecord and report a warning rather than undo durable capture.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL
          14.12. AC: Vector Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL If asynchronous vector ingestion cannot be scheduled after capture, the .ragmem body, metadata, and SQLite row shall remain saved.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION
      5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-INPUT
    5.10. Capture Input Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-INPUT capture_memory_pair shall return unsuccessful status and create no record when input_text is empty after trimming.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION
      5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.

    5.11. Capture Output Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-OUTPUT capture_memory_pair shall return unsuccessful status and create no record when output_text is empty after trimming.

      5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.

    5.13. Captured Record Defaults GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-DEFAULTS MemoryManager.capture_pair shall create the new record with tag Green, retrieval_source_mode QA, and empty direct_recall_key.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION
      5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

      5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.

        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-PERSIST-SEQUENCE
    5.16. Capture Persistence Sequence GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-PERSIST-SEQUENCE After ActiveBrief processing, MemoryManager shall append the record to its in-memory list, append one record block to .ragmem, rewrite .ragmeta.json, and refresh SQLite.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CAPTURE
      14.3. AC: Durable Pair Capture GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CAPTURE Given non-empty input and output, capture shall produce one MemoryRecord, append one stable .ragmem block, update .ragmeta.json, and mirror the record in SQLite.

      5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.

        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    5.17. Capture Result GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-RETURN A successful capture action shall return success, the MemoryRecord, record_id, file_id, and active .ragmem filename.

      5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE
    5.18. Optional Ingestion Scheduling GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE When a memory_ingestion_manager is supplied, capture_memory_pair shall attempt to schedule asynchronous ingestion after durable capture completes.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL
      5.19. Ingestion Scheduling Failure GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL If vector-ingestion scheduling raises an exception, the action shall retain the saved MemoryRecord and report a warning rather than undo durable capture.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL
        14.12. AC: Vector Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL If asynchronous vector ingestion cannot be scheduled after capture, the .ragmem body, metadata, and SQLite row shall remain saved.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE
      5.18. Optional Ingestion Scheduling GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE When a memory_ingestion_manager is supplied, capture_memory_pair shall attempt to schedule asynchronous ingestion after durable capture completes.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION
        5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL
    5.19. Ingestion Scheduling Failure GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL If vector-ingestion scheduling raises an exception, the action shall retain the saved MemoryRecord and report a warning rather than undo durable capture.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL
      14.12. AC: Vector Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL If asynchronous vector ingestion cannot be scheduled after capture, the .ragmem body, metadata, and SQLite row shall remain saved.

    6. 6. Stable .ragmem Body Persistence
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY
    6.1. .ragmem Stable Body Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY The .ragmem file shall remain the authority for stable MemoryRecord body fields.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MARKERS
      6.2. Record Block Markers GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MARKERS Each .ragmem record block shall be delimited by the exact current start and end markers defined by MemoryRecord.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-JSON
      6.3. Record Block JSON GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-JSON The content between record markers shall be one UTF-8 JSON object serialized with ensure_ascii disabled and indentation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-FIELDS
      6.4. Stable Body Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-FIELDS The current .ragmem body shall contain record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, input_text, output_text, source, input_hash, output_hash, active_retrieval_brief_title, active_retrieval_brief, and active_retrieval_brief_contributor_ids.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-EDITABLE
      6.5. Editable Metadata Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-EDITABLE Current tag, retrieval_source_mode, direct_recall_key, automatic keywords, user keywords, active project name, and embedded-file snapshot shall not be duplicated into newly written body-only .ragmem blocks.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-APPEND
      6.6. Record Append Behavior GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-APPEND Normal pair capture shall open the active .ragmem file in append mode, write one complete MemoryRecord block, and preserve existing blocks.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-CREATED-FLAG
        6.7. File-Created Flag GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-CREATED-FLAG After a record block is appended, MemoryManager shall mark b_file_created true.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ
      6.8. Record Block Loading GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ Loading a history shall locate marker-delimited blocks, parse JSON objects, and construct MemoryRecords in file order.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MALFORMED-BLOCK
        6.9. Malformed Block Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MALFORMED-BLOCK A marker-delimited block that cannot be parsed into a JSON object shall be skipped by the current loader without preventing other valid blocks from loading.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-BACKWARD
        6.10. Older Full-Block Compatibility GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-BACKWARD MemoryRecord.from_dict shall accept older .ragmem blocks that contain metadata in addition to stable body fields; current .ragmeta.json metadata shall be overlaid later when available.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-VECTORS
      6.11. No Vector Data in .ragmem GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-VECTORS The .ragmem body shall not store embedding vectors, similarity scores, runtime candidates, compression output, or synthesized Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY
      6.1. .ragmem Stable Body Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY The .ragmem file shall remain the authority for stable MemoryRecord body fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MARKERS
    6.2. Record Block Markers GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MARKERS Each .ragmem record block shall be delimited by the exact current start and end markers defined by MemoryRecord.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY

    6.4. Stable Body Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-FIELDS The current .ragmem body shall contain record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, input_text, output_text, source, input_hash, output_hash, active_retrieval_brief_title, active_retrieval_brief, and active_retrieval_brief_contributor_ids.

      6.1. .ragmem Stable Body Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY The .ragmem file shall remain the authority for stable MemoryRecord body fields.

    GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-APPEND
    6.6. Record Append Behavior GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-APPEND Normal pair capture shall open the active .ragmem file in append mode, write one complete MemoryRecord block, and preserve existing blocks.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-CREATED-FLAG
      6.7. File-Created Flag GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-CREATED-FLAG After a record block is appended, MemoryManager shall mark b_file_created true.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-APPEND
      6.6. Record Append Behavior GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-APPEND Normal pair capture shall open the active .ragmem file in append mode, write one complete MemoryRecord block, and preserve existing blocks.

    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY
      6.1. .ragmem Stable Body Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY The .ragmem file shall remain the authority for stable MemoryRecord body fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ
    6.8. Record Block Loading GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ Loading a history shall locate marker-delimited blocks, parse JSON objects, and construct MemoryRecords in file order.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MALFORMED-BLOCK
      6.9. Malformed Block Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MALFORMED-BLOCK A marker-delimited block that cannot be parsed into a JSON object shall be skipped by the current loader without preventing other valid blocks from loading.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-BACKWARD
      6.10. Older Full-Block Compatibility GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-BACKWARD MemoryRecord.from_dict shall accept older .ragmem blocks that contain metadata in addition to stable body fields; current .ragmeta.json metadata shall be overlaid later when available.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ
      6.8. Record Block Loading GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ Loading a history shall locate marker-delimited blocks, parse JSON objects, and construct MemoryRecords in file order.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY
        6.1. .ragmem Stable Body Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY The .ragmem file shall remain the authority for stable MemoryRecord body fields.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MALFORMED-BLOCK
    6.9. Malformed Block Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-MALFORMED-BLOCK A marker-delimited block that cannot be parsed into a JSON object shall be skipped by the current loader without preventing other valid blocks from loading.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ
      6.8. Record Block Loading GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-READ Loading a history shall locate marker-delimited blocks, parse JSON objects, and construct MemoryRecords in file order.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY
        6.1. .ragmem Stable Body Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY The .ragmem file shall remain the authority for stable MemoryRecord body fields.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-BACKWARD
    6.10. Older Full-Block Compatibility GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-BACKWARD MemoryRecord.from_dict shall accept older .ragmem blocks that contain metadata in addition to stable body fields; current .ragmeta.json metadata shall be overlaid later when available.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY
      6.1. .ragmem Stable Body Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-AUTHORITY The .ragmem file shall remain the authority for stable MemoryRecord body fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-VECTORS
    6.11. No Vector Data in .ragmem GHOST-MEM-REC-RAGMEM-NO-VECTORS The .ragmem body shall not store embedding vectors, similarity scores, runtime candidates, compression output, or synthesized Memory Context.

    7. 7. .ragmeta.json Metadata Persistence
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
    7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-REBUILD
      7.2. Metadata Rebuild from Records GHOST-MEM-REC-META-REBUILD MemoryManager.save_metainfo shall rebuild metainfo from the current in-memory record list before writing the file.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS
      7.3. File-Level Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS The current metadata file shall include file_id, title, paired filenames, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, record_count, record_ids, parent_ids, tag_summary, aggregate automatic keywords, aggregate user keywords, and per-record metadata views.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-CREATED
        7.4. Metadata Created Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-META-CREATED For a non-empty history, created_at_utc shall be taken from the first MemoryRecord; for an empty in-memory history built by MemoryManager it may be empty.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-UPDATED
        7.5. Metadata Updated Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-META-UPDATED For a non-empty history, updated_at_utc shall be refreshed to current UTC when metainfo is rebuilt.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AGGREGATES
        7.6. Metadata Aggregate Deduplication GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AGGREGATES Aggregate parent identifiers and keyword lists shall be deduplicated while preserving first occurrence order.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-RECORD-VIEW
      7.7. Per-Record Metadata View GHOST-MEM-REC-META-RECORD-VIEW Each per-record metadata entry shall use MemoryRecord.to_index_dict and shall not duplicate full input/output or full ActiveBrief text.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-OVERLAY
      7.8. Metadata Overlay on Load GHOST-MEM-REC-META-OVERLAY When loading a history with an existing metadata file, MemoryManager shall apply metadata entries to matching record_id values after loading stable bodies from .ragmem.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-STABLE-OVERWRITE
        7.9. Stable Field Overlay Protection GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-STABLE-OVERWRITE Metadata overlay shall not modify record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, input/output text, source, hashes, or stable ActiveBrief fields.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-AUTHORITY
          14.5. AC: Body and Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-AUTHORITY Editing a tag shall change .ragmeta.json and SQLite metadata while leaving the corresponding .ragmem stable body unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MISSING
      7.10. Missing Metadata File GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MISSING When a history is loaded and its .ragmeta.json file is missing, MemoryManager shall rebuild and save metainfo from loaded records.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MALFORMED
      7.11. Malformed Metadata File GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MALFORMED Malformed .ragmeta.json input shall not be silently interpreted as valid metadata by MemoryManager.load_history; the JSON loading failure shall remain visible to the caller.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-RUNTIME
      7.12. Runtime Artifact Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-RUNTIME The metadata file shall not store raw vector hits, MemoryContextPack, compression results, synthesis diagnostics, or synthesized Memory Context.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-NO-RUNTIME-PERSIST
        14.13. AC: Runtime Artifact Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-NO-RUNTIME-PERSIST MemoryContextPack, query-dependent compression, and synthesized Memory Context shall not appear in newly written .ragmem or .ragmeta.json data.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
      7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-META-REBUILD
    7.2. Metadata Rebuild from Records GHOST-MEM-REC-META-REBUILD MemoryManager.save_metainfo shall rebuild metainfo from the current in-memory record list before writing the file.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
      7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS
    7.3. File-Level Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS The current metadata file shall include file_id, title, paired filenames, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, record_count, record_ids, parent_ids, tag_summary, aggregate automatic keywords, aggregate user keywords, and per-record metadata views.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-CREATED
      7.4. Metadata Created Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-META-CREATED For a non-empty history, created_at_utc shall be taken from the first MemoryRecord; for an empty in-memory history built by MemoryManager it may be empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-UPDATED
      7.5. Metadata Updated Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-META-UPDATED For a non-empty history, updated_at_utc shall be refreshed to current UTC when metainfo is rebuilt.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AGGREGATES
      7.6. Metadata Aggregate Deduplication GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AGGREGATES Aggregate parent identifiers and keyword lists shall be deduplicated while preserving first occurrence order.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS
      7.3. File-Level Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS The current metadata file shall include file_id, title, paired filenames, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, record_count, record_ids, parent_ids, tag_summary, aggregate automatic keywords, aggregate user keywords, and per-record metadata views.

    7.4. Metadata Created Timestamp GHOST-MEM-REC-META-CREATED For a non-empty history, created_at_utc shall be taken from the first MemoryRecord; for an empty in-memory history built by MemoryManager it may be empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS
      7.3. File-Level Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS The current metadata file shall include file_id, title, paired filenames, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, record_count, record_ids, parent_ids, tag_summary, aggregate automatic keywords, aggregate user keywords, and per-record metadata views.

      7.3. File-Level Metadata Fields GHOST-MEM-REC-META-FILE-FIELDS The current metadata file shall include file_id, title, paired filenames, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, record_count, record_ids, parent_ids, tag_summary, aggregate automatic keywords, aggregate user keywords, and per-record metadata views.

        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

      14.5. AC: Body and Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-AUTHORITY Editing a tag shall change .ragmeta.json and SQLite metadata while leaving the corresponding .ragmem stable body unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
      7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MISSING
    7.10. Missing Metadata File GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MISSING When a history is loaded and its .ragmeta.json file is missing, MemoryManager shall rebuild and save metainfo from loaded records.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
      7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MALFORMED
    7.11. Malformed Metadata File GHOST-MEM-REC-META-MALFORMED Malformed .ragmeta.json input shall not be silently interpreted as valid metadata by MemoryManager.load_history; the JSON loading failure shall remain visible to the caller.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
      7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-RUNTIME
    7.12. Runtime Artifact Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-RUNTIME The metadata file shall not store raw vector hits, MemoryContextPack, compression results, synthesis diagnostics, or synthesized Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-NO-RUNTIME-PERSIST
      14.13. AC: Runtime Artifact Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-NO-RUNTIME-PERSIST MemoryContextPack, query-dependent compression, and synthesized Memory Context shall not appear in newly written .ragmem or .ragmeta.json data.

    8. 8. SQLite Metadata Mirror
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
    8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-PK
      8.2. Memory File Primary Key GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-PK memory_files.file_id shall be the table primary key.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-PK
      8.3. Memory Record Composite Key GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-PK memory_records shall use the composite primary key file_id and record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-COLUMNS
      8.4. File Table Columns GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-COLUMNS memory_files shall store file_id, title, filename_ragmem, filename_meta, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, and record_count.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-COLUMNS
      8.5. Record Table Columns GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-COLUMNS memory_records shall store file_id, record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, source, tag, retrieval_source_mode, direct_recall_key, keyword JSON, project/file snapshot JSON, and input/output hashes.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-NO-BODY
      8.6. No Full Q/A in SQLite GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-NO-BODY memory_records shall not store full input_text, output_text, or full ActiveRetrievalBrief text.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-FILE
      8.7. File Row Upsert GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-FILE refresh_sqlite_index shall insert or update the active memory_files row by file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-RECORD
      8.8. Record Row Upsert GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-RECORD refresh_sqlite_index shall insert or update every current MemoryRecord row by file_id and record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIRROR-DELETE
      8.9. Mirror Row Cleanup GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIRROR-DELETE After upserting the active record list, SQLite rows for the active file_id whose record_id is no longer present shall be deleted.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-EMPTY-CLEAN
        8.10. Empty Active Record Cleanup GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-EMPTY-CLEAN When the active MemoryManager record list is empty, refresh shall delete all memory_records rows for the active file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-TAG
      8.11. Tag Index GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-TAG The current schema shall create an index on memory_records.tag.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-PROJECT
      8.12. Project Index GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-PROJECT The current schema shall create an index on memory_records.active_project_name.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-KEY
      8.13. Direct Recall Key Index GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-INDEX-KEY The current schema shall create an index on memory_records.direct_recall_key.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIGRATION
      8.14. Current Column Migration GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIGRATION Schema initialization shall add retrieval_source_mode and direct_recall_key columns with current defaults when loading an older memory_records table that lacks them.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-LIST
      8.15. History Listing Order GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-LIST MemoryManager.list_histories shall return memory_files rows ordered by updated_at_utc descending.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-PK
    8.2. Memory File Primary Key GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-PK memory_files.file_id shall be the table primary key.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-PK
    8.3. Memory Record Composite Key GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-PK memory_records shall use the composite primary key file_id and record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-COLUMNS
    8.4. File Table Columns GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-FILE-COLUMNS memory_files shall store file_id, title, filename_ragmem, filename_meta, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, and record_count.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-COLUMNS
    8.5. Record Table Columns GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-RECORD-COLUMNS memory_records shall store file_id, record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, source, tag, retrieval_source_mode, direct_recall_key, keyword JSON, project/file snapshot JSON, and input/output hashes.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-NO-BODY
    8.6. No Full Q/A in SQLite GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-NO-BODY memory_records shall not store full input_text, output_text, or full ActiveRetrievalBrief text.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.

        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-FILE
    8.7. File Row Upsert GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-FILE refresh_sqlite_index shall insert or update the active memory_files row by file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-RECORD
    8.8. Record Row Upsert GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-UPsert-RECORD refresh_sqlite_index shall insert or update every current MemoryRecord row by file_id and record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA
      8.1. SQLite Schema Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-SCHEMA MemoryManager shall create memory_files and memory_records tables when absent.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIRROR-DELETE
    8.9. Mirror Row Cleanup GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIRROR-DELETE After upserting the active record list, SQLite rows for the active file_id whose record_id is no longer present shall be deleted.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-EMPTY-CLEAN
      8.10. Empty Active Record Cleanup GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-EMPTY-CLEAN When the active MemoryManager record list is empty, refresh shall delete all memory_records rows for the active file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIRROR-DELETE
      8.9. Mirror Row Cleanup GHOST-MEM-REC-SQLITE-MIRROR-DELETE After upserting the active record list, SQLite rows for the active file_id whose record_id is no longer present shall be deleted.

    9. 9. Loading, Closing, and Active State
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-ID
    9.1. History Lookup by file_id GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-ID MemoryManager.load_history shall resolve the requested history through memory_files.file_id and shall fail when no matching row exists.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-FIELDS
      9.2. Loaded File-Level State GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-FIELDS Loading shall set file_id, title, filename_ragmem, and filename_meta from the SQLite file row.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-RECORDS
      9.3. Loaded Record State GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-RECORDS Loading shall replace the current record list with MemoryRecords read from the selected .ragmem file.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-PENDING-RESET
      9.4. Pending Buffer Reset on Load GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-PENDING-RESET Loading a history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-CREATED-FLAG
      9.5. Loaded File-Created Flag GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-CREATED-FLAG After load, b_file_created shall indicate whether the selected .ragmem path exists.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-REFRESH
      9.6. SQLite Refresh after Load GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-REFRESH After loading bodies and metadata, MemoryManager shall refresh the SQLite mirror for the active history.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-LOAD
        14.6. AC: History Load GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-LOAD Given a valid indexed history, loading shall restore record order and stable bodies, overlay current metadata, reset pending-topic state, and refresh SQLite.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-ID
      9.1. History Lookup by file_id GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-ID MemoryManager.load_history shall resolve the requested history through memory_files.file_id and shall fail when no matching row exists.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.

    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-LOAD
      14.6. AC: History Load GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-LOAD Given a valid indexed history, loading shall restore record order and stable bodies, overlay current metadata, reset pending-topic state, and refresh SQLite.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-CLOSE
    9.7. MemoryManager Close GHOST-MEM-REC-CLOSE MemoryManager.close shall save current metainfo and refresh the SQLite index.

    10. 10. Memory History File Operations
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-LIST
    10.1. List Histories GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-LIST MemoryFileManager.list_histories shall return the MemoryManager SQLite history listing.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE
    10.2. Create History Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE MemoryFileManager.create_history shall reject an empty title.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE
      10.3. Create Empty History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE Creating an empty history shall make it active, create an empty .ragmem file, create paired .ragmeta.json content, and create or update the memory_files row with record_count zero.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TIMES
        10.4. Empty History Timestamps GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TIMES An explicitly created empty history shall use current UTC for both created_at_utc and updated_at_utc in its initial metadata.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CREATE
        14.7. AC: Empty History Creation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CREATE Creating a named empty history shall produce paired empty/body metadata files and a file-level SQLite row with zero records.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE
      10.2. Create History Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE MemoryFileManager.create_history shall reject an empty title.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE
    10.3. Create Empty History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE Creating an empty history shall make it active, create an empty .ragmem file, create paired .ragmeta.json content, and create or update the memory_files row with record_count zero.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TIMES
      10.4. Empty History Timestamps GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TIMES An explicitly created empty history shall use current UTC for both created_at_utc and updated_at_utc in its initial metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CREATE
      14.7. AC: Empty History Creation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CREATE Creating a named empty history shall produce paired empty/body metadata files and a file-level SQLite row with zero records.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE
      10.3. Create Empty History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE Creating an empty history shall make it active, create an empty .ragmem file, create paired .ragmeta.json content, and create or update the memory_files row with record_count zero.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE
        10.2. Create History Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE MemoryFileManager.create_history shall reject an empty title.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TIMES
    10.4. Empty History Timestamps GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TIMES An explicitly created empty history shall use current UTC for both created_at_utc and updated_at_utc in its initial metadata.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-LOAD
    10.5. File Manager Load GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-LOAD MemoryFileManager.load_history shall validate file_id and delegate active-history loading to MemoryManager.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE
    10.6. Rename Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE MemoryFileManager.rename_history shall reject an empty file_id or new title and shall reject unknown file_id values.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ID
      10.7. Rename Preserves file_id GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ID Renaming a memory history shall preserve file_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-RENAME
        14.8. AC: Stable Rename Identity GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-RENAME Renaming a history shall preserve file_id while updating paired filenames, title, metadata file fields, and the SQLite file row.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-PAIR
      10.8. Rename Paired Files GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-PAIR Renaming shall rename the physical .ragmem and .ragmeta.json files when they exist and update their stored filename fields.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TIMESTAMP
      10.9. Rename Timestamp Prefix GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TIMESTAMP Renaming shall preserve an existing YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM filename prefix when present; otherwise it shall use the current minute prefix.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-COLLISION
      10.10. Rename Collision Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-COLLISION When either target filename already exists, rename shall append the first eight characters of file_id to the new stem.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-SQLITE
      10.11. Rename SQLite Update GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-SQLITE Renaming shall update title, paired filenames, and updated_at_utc in memory_files.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ACTIVE
      10.12. Rename Active History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ACTIVE When the renamed history is active, MemoryManager file-level state shall be updated and its metadata and SQLite mirror shall be refreshed.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-INACTIVE
      10.13. Rename Inactive History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-INACTIVE When the renamed history is not active, the renamed metadata file shall have its file-level title, paired filenames, and updated timestamp updated without loading the history.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE
      10.6. Rename Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE MemoryFileManager.rename_history shall reject an empty file_id or new title and shall reject unknown file_id values.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.

    10.10. Rename Collision Handling GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-COLLISION When either target filename already exists, rename shall append the first eight characters of file_id to the new stem.

        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

      10.6. Rename Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE MemoryFileManager.rename_history shall reject an empty file_id or new title and shall reject unknown file_id values.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

    10.13. Rename Inactive History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-INACTIVE When the renamed history is not active, the renamed metadata file shall have its file-level title, paired filenames, and updated timestamp updated without loading the history.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE
    10.14. Delete History Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE MemoryFileManager.delete_history shall reject an empty or unknown file_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VECTORS
      10.15. Delete History Vectors GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VECTORS When a compatible MemoryVectorStore is supplied, deleting a history shall request deletion of vectors by file_id before deleting SQLite rows and files.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-SQLITE
      10.16. Delete History SQLite Rows GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-SQLITE Deleting a history shall remove matching rows from memory_records and memory_files.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-PHYSICAL
      10.17. Delete History Physical Files GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-PHYSICAL Deleting a history shall unlink its .ragmem and .ragmeta.json files when present.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-DELETE
        14.9. AC: Complete History Delete GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-DELETE Deleting a selected history shall remove its SQLite rows, physical paired files, and memory vectors when the vector store is connected.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-ACTIVE
      10.18. Delete Active History State GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-ACTIVE When the deleted history is active, the current implementation shall replace file_id, clear title and filenames, clear records and metainfo, and mark b_file_created false.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-REPORT
      10.19. Delete History Report GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-REPORT The delete result shall report file identity, prior filenames, title, and number of vectors deleted when available.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-DELETE-CONFIRM
      10.20. Typed Delete Confirmation GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-DELETE-CONFIRM The current Streamlit file action shall require the exact trimmed text delete before confirming history deletion.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE
      10.14. Delete History Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE MemoryFileManager.delete_history shall reject an empty or unknown file_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VECTORS
    10.15. Delete History Vectors GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VECTORS When a compatible MemoryVectorStore is supplied, deleting a history shall request deletion of vectors by file_id before deleting SQLite rows and files.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE
