    11. 11. ChatGPT Shared-Link Import
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-URL
    11.1. Shared URL Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-URL The current importer shall accept only a non-empty URL matching https://chatgpt.com/share/<conversation-id> with the supported identifier characters and optional query string.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-TITLE
    11.2. Import Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-TITLE The current importer shall require a non-empty title.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PLAYWRIGHT
    11.3. Playwright Dependency GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PLAYWRIGHT Current shared-page extraction shall use Playwright Chromium and shall report an explicit dependency error when Playwright is unavailable.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-NAVIGATION
    11.4. Shared Page Navigation GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-NAVIGATION The importer shall navigate with domcontentloaded, bounded timeout, and current retry handling for specified transient network errors.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-EXTRACTION-ORDER
    11.5. Message Extraction Sources GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-EXTRACTION-ORDER The importer shall attempt visible DOM message extraction and embedded structured-message extraction and shall retain body text as fallback input.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-STABILITY
    11.6. Message Stability Wait GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-STABILITY When enabled, current extraction shall scroll and wait for visible message count stability before selecting the message source.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-ROLES
    11.7. Supported Imported Roles GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-ROLES Only user and assistant messages shall participate in Q/A pair creation.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PAIRING
    11.8. Imported Message Pairing GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PAIRING Ordered user content followed by assistant content shall be combined into one input/output pair; a later user message shall flush the completed prior pair.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RAW-FALLBACK
    11.9. Raw Text Pair Fallback GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RAW-FALLBACK When no structured Q/A pair is produced but cleaned body text exists, the importer shall create one pair whose input identifies the imported conversation and whose output is the body text.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT-FALLBACK
      14.11. AC: Import Body Fallback GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT-FALLBACK Given no structured Q/A pairs but usable body text, import shall create exactly one fallback pair rather than silently report a structured extraction success.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-NO-CONTENT
    11.10. No Importable Content GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-NO-CONTENT When neither pairs nor usable fallback text exist, the import shall fail explicitly.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RECORDS
    11.11. Imported MemoryRecord Construction GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RECORDS Each imported pair shall become a normal MemoryRecord with source prefixed chatgpt_shared_link:, tag Green, retrieval source mode QA, empty Direct Recall Key, and user keyword chatgpt_shared_link.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PARENT-CHAIN
      11.12. Imported Parent Chain GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PARENT-CHAIN Imported records shall set each record parent_id to the immediately preceding imported record_id, with the first parent_id empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT
      11.13. Imported Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT The importer shall apply the supplied active project name and embedded-file snapshot to each imported record.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RECORDS
      11.11. Imported MemoryRecord Construction GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RECORDS Each imported pair shall become a normal MemoryRecord with source prefixed chatgpt_shared_link:, tag Green, retrieval source mode QA, empty Direct Recall Key, and user keyword chatgpt_shared_link.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PARENT-CHAIN
    11.12. Imported Parent Chain GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PARENT-CHAIN Imported records shall set each record parent_id to the immediately preceding imported record_id, with the first parent_id empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RECORDS
      11.11. Imported MemoryRecord Construction GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RECORDS Each imported pair shall become a normal MemoryRecord with source prefixed chatgpt_shared_link:, tag Green, retrieval source mode QA, empty Direct Recall Key, and user keyword chatgpt_shared_link.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT
    11.13. Imported Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT The importer shall apply the supplied active project name and embedded-file snapshot to each imported record.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-BRIEF
    11.14. Optional Shared Memory Brief GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-BRIEF When a non-empty import memory_brief is supplied, the importer shall assign that brief and its title to every imported record and shall use all imported record IDs as contributor IDs.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST
    11.15. Imported History Persistence GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST Imported records shall be persisted as a new ordinary memory history by writing all record blocks to a new .ragmem file, saving metadata, and refreshing SQLite.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PENDING-RESET
      11.16. Imported Pending Buffer Reset GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PENDING-RESET Persisting an imported history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-INGEST
      11.17. Optional Imported Vector Ingestion GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-INGEST When MemoryIngestionManager is available, the importer shall call ingest_all after durable history persistence.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT
      14.10. AC: Shared Conversation Import GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT Given a valid public shared conversation with importable user/assistant content, import shall create a new ordinary memory history whose Q/A pairs are normal MemoryRecords and may be vector-ingested after persistence.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST
      11.15. Imported History Persistence GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST Imported records shall be persisted as a new ordinary memory history by writing all record blocks to a new .ragmem file, saving metadata, and refreshing SQLite.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PENDING-RESET
    11.16. Imported Pending Buffer Reset GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PENDING-RESET Persisting an imported history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to empty.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST
      11.15. Imported History Persistence GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST Imported records shall be persisted as a new ordinary memory history by writing all record blocks to a new .ragmem file, saving metadata, and refreshing SQLite.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-INGEST
    11.17. Optional Imported Vector Ingestion GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-INGEST When MemoryIngestionManager is available, the importer shall call ingest_all after durable history persistence.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-REPORT
    11.18. Import Result Report GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-REPORT A successful import shall report URL, title, file identity, paired filenames, record/pair/message counts, raw text character count, extraction method, brief assignment status, and optional vector-ingestion result.

    12. 12. Streamlit Session and Action Wiring
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-MANAGER
    12.1. Session MemoryManager Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-MANAGER Current Streamlit startup shall create one MemoryManager in session state with data/memory as memory_root and memory_index.sqlite3 as sqlite_path.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-CHUNKER
    12.2. Session MemoryChunker Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-CHUNKER Current Streamlit startup shall create one MemoryChunker in session state when absent.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-STORE
    12.3. Session MemoryVectorStore Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-STORE Current Streamlit startup shall create one MemoryVectorStore in session state under data/memory/vector_db with collection memory_vectors.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-INGEST
    12.4. Session MemoryIngestionManager Initialization GHOST-MEM-REC-SESSION-INGEST Current Streamlit startup shall construct MemoryIngestionManager from the session MemoryManager, MemoryChunker, and MemoryVectorStore.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-MANUAL-FEED
    12.5. Manual Memory Feed GHOST-MEM-REC-MANUAL-FEED The current manual memory-feed action shall read prompt_text and manual_memory_feed_text, reject either empty value, capture active project/file snapshot, collect editable record metadata, and call capture_memory_pair with source manual_memory_feed.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILES-ACTIONS
    12.6. Files Page Actions GHOST-MEM-REC-FILES-ACTIONS The current files action layer shall delegate create, load, rename, delete, and import operations to MemoryFileManager or the ChatGPT importer rather than implement persistence directly.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-WIDGET-CLEAR
    12.7. Memory Widget State Reset GHOST-MEM-REC-WIDGET-CLEAR After file create, load, delete, or import, the current UI shall clear per-record tag, retrieval-mode, user-keyword, and Direct Recall widget state keys.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVE-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT
    12.8. UI Active Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVE-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT When an active project exists, the current UI shall attempt to capture the project name and controller-reported embedded file list for new or imported records.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-NO-PROJECT
      12.9. No Active Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-NO-PROJECT When no active project exists, current capture/import wiring shall use no project name and an empty embedded-file snapshot.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVE-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT
      12.8. UI Active Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-ACTIVE-PROJECT-SNAPSHOT When an active project exists, the current UI shall attempt to capture the project name and controller-reported embedded file list for new or imported records.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-NO-PROJECT
    12.9. No Active Project Snapshot GHOST-MEM-REC-UI-NO-PROJECT When no active project exists, current capture/import wiring shall use no project name and an empty embedded-file snapshot.

    13. 13. Error Handling, Security, and Quality
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-EMPTY-PAIR
    13.1. No Empty Memory Pair GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-EMPTY-PAIR The recording action shall not create a MemoryRecord from an empty prompt or empty response.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-EMPTY
      14.4. AC: Empty Pair Rejection GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-EMPTY Given empty input or output after trimming, no record, file append, metadata rewrite, SQLite row, or vector-ingestion schedule shall be produced.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-ID-VALIDATION
    13.2. File Identifier Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-ID-VALIDATION File-level operations shall reject empty file_id values rather than execute broad file or database operations.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-SANITATION
    13.3. Filename Path Sanitization GHOST-MEM-REC-PATH-SANITATION User-provided history titles shall not be used directly as path fragments; the current safe-title transformation shall be applied.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE-PRESERVATION
    13.4. Source Preservation GHOST-MEM-REC-SOURCE-PRESERVATION Memory recording and import shall preserve source metadata sufficient to distinguish manual capture from imported shared conversations.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-HASH-INTEGRITY
    13.5. Body Hash Integrity GHOST-MEM-REC-HASH-INTEGRITY New MemoryRecords shall compute input/output hashes from exact stored text and shall preserve loaded persisted hashes when present.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-SECRET
    13.6. No Secret Persistence GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-SECRET Memory body and metadata persistence shall not intentionally store provider API keys or browser credentials.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-PUBLIC-LOG
    13.7. Public Recording Logs GHOST-MEM-REC-PUBLIC-LOG Public recording logs shall use concise file or record identifiers and status messages and shall not emit full MemoryRecord bodies by default.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-CONFIDENTIAL-LOG
    13.8. Confidential Developer Logs GHOST-MEM-REC-CONFIDENTIAL-LOG Complete memory payloads or detailed importer diagnostics shall remain behind explicitly enabled confidential developer logging.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-ORDER
    13.9. Delete Ordering GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-ORDER Current history deletion shall attempt vector cleanup, then delete SQLite rows, then unlink physical files, preserving the actual execution order for diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-VECTOR-TRUTH
    13.10. No Vector Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-VECTOR-TRUTH Successful or failed vector ingestion shall not change which stable input/output body is authoritative.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-TESTABILITY
    13.11. Recording Testability GHOST-MEM-REC-TESTABILITY MemoryRecord serialization, title sanitation, metadata rebuild, SQLite mirroring, load overlay, and file operations shall be testable without provider LLM calls.

    14. 14. Traceability and Acceptance Criteria
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-CODE-OWNERSHIP
    14.1. Recording Code Ownership GHOST-MEM-REC-CODE-OWNERSHIP MemoryRecord, MemoryManager, memory actions, file manager, import helpers, and file UI actions shall map to the deepest applicable UIDs in this document.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-SYNC
      14.14. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-SYNC A recording baseline shall not be accepted when stable-body fields, metadata authority, file identity, capture order, SQLite mirror semantics, or history operations contradict this document.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-SIBLING-BOUNDARY
    14.2. Sibling Ownership Boundary GHOST-MEM-REC-SIBLING-BOUNDARY Memory vector ingestion and retrieval shall remain owned by 52; ActiveBrief algorithms, runtime compression, and synthesis shall remain owned by 53.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-PERSIST-SEQUENCE
      5.16. Capture Persistence Sequence GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-PERSIST-SEQUENCE After ActiveBrief processing, MemoryManager shall append the record to its in-memory list, append one record block to .ragmem, rewrite .ragmeta.json, and refresh SQLite.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION
        5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CAPTURE
    14.3. AC: Durable Pair Capture GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CAPTURE Given non-empty input and output, capture shall produce one MemoryRecord, append one stable .ragmem block, update .ragmeta.json, and mirror the record in SQLite.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-EMPTY-PAIR
      13.1. No Empty Memory Pair GHOST-MEM-REC-NO-EMPTY-PAIR The recording action shall not create a MemoryRecord from an empty prompt or empty response.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-EMPTY
    14.4. AC: Empty Pair Rejection GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-EMPTY Given empty input or output after trimming, no record, file append, metadata rewrite, SQLite row, or vector-ingestion schedule shall be produced.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-STABLE-OVERWRITE
      7.9. Stable Field Overlay Protection GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-STABLE-OVERWRITE Metadata overlay shall not modify record_id, parent_id, created_at_utc, input/output text, source, hashes, or stable ActiveBrief fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-OVERLAY
        7.8. Metadata Overlay on Load GHOST-MEM-REC-META-OVERLAY When loading a history with an existing metadata file, MemoryManager shall apply metadata entries to matching record_id values after loading stable bodies from .ragmem.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
          7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
          • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
            1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-AUTHORITY
    14.5. AC: Body and Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-AUTHORITY Editing a tag shall change .ragmeta.json and SQLite metadata while leaving the corresponding .ragmem stable body unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-REFRESH
      9.6. SQLite Refresh after Load GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-REFRESH After loading bodies and metadata, MemoryManager shall refresh the SQLite mirror for the active history.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-ID
        9.1. History Lookup by file_id GHOST-MEM-REC-LOAD-ID MemoryManager.load_history shall resolve the requested history through memory_files.file_id and shall fail when no matching row exists.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-LOAD
    14.6. AC: History Load GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-LOAD Given a valid indexed history, loading shall restore record order and stable bodies, overlay current metadata, reset pending-topic state, and refresh SQLite.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE
      10.3. Create Empty History GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE Creating an empty history shall make it active, create an empty .ragmem file, create paired .ragmeta.json content, and create or update the memory_files row with record_count zero.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE
        10.2. Create History Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-CREATE-TITLE MemoryFileManager.create_history shall reject an empty title.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CREATE
    14.7. AC: Empty History Creation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-CREATE Creating a named empty history shall produce paired empty/body metadata files and a file-level SQLite row with zero records.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ID
      10.7. Rename Preserves file_id GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-ID Renaming a memory history shall preserve file_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE
        10.6. Rename Title Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-RENAME-TITLE MemoryFileManager.rename_history shall reject an empty file_id or new title and shall reject unknown file_id values.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-RENAME
    14.8. AC: Stable Rename Identity GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-RENAME Renaming a history shall preserve file_id while updating paired filenames, title, metadata file fields, and the SQLite file row.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-PHYSICAL
      10.17. Delete History Physical Files GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-PHYSICAL Deleting a history shall unlink its .ragmem and .ragmeta.json files when present.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE
        10.14. Delete History Validation GHOST-MEM-REC-FILE-DELETE-VALIDATE MemoryFileManager.delete_history shall reject an empty or unknown file_id.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-DELETE
    14.9. AC: Complete History Delete GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-DELETE Deleting a selected history shall remove its SQLite rows, physical paired files, and memory vectors when the vector store is connected.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST
      11.15. Imported History Persistence GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-PERSIST Imported records shall be persisted as a new ordinary memory history by writing all record blocks to a new .ragmem file, saving metadata, and refreshing SQLite.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT
    14.10. AC: Shared Conversation Import GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT Given a valid public shared conversation with importable user/assistant content, import shall create a new ordinary memory history whose Q/A pairs are normal MemoryRecords and may be vector-ingested after persistence.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RAW-FALLBACK
      11.9. Raw Text Pair Fallback GHOST-MEM-REC-IMPORT-RAW-FALLBACK When no structured Q/A pair is produced but cleaned body text exists, the importer shall create one pair whose input identifies the imported conversation and whose output is the body text.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT-FALLBACK
    14.11. AC: Import Body Fallback GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-IMPORT-FALLBACK Given no structured Q/A pairs but usable body text, import shall create exactly one fallback pair rather than silently report a structured extraction success.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL
      5.19. Ingestion Scheduling Failure GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE-FAIL If vector-ingestion scheduling raises an exception, the action shall retain the saved MemoryRecord and report a warning rather than undo durable capture.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE
        5.18. Optional Ingestion Scheduling GHOST-MEM-REC-INGEST-SCHEDULE When a memory_ingestion_manager is supplied, capture_memory_pair shall attempt to schedule asynchronous ingestion after durable capture completes.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION
          5.9. Reusable Capture Action GHOST-MEM-REC-CAPTURE-ACTION capture_memory_pair shall provide the reusable workflow used by manual memory feed and other callers.
          • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
            1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL
    14.12. AC: Vector Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-VECTOR-FAIL If asynchronous vector ingestion cannot be scheduled after capture, the .ragmem body, metadata, and SQLite row shall remain saved.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-RUNTIME
      7.12. Runtime Artifact Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-META-NO-RUNTIME The metadata file shall not store raw vector hits, MemoryContextPack, compression results, synthesis diagnostics, or synthesized Memory Context.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY
        7.1. .ragmeta.json Metadata Authority GHOST-MEM-REC-META-AUTHORITY The paired .ragmeta.json file shall represent current editable/readable metadata for one memory history.
        • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-NO-RUNTIME-PERSIST
    14.13. AC: Runtime Artifact Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-NO-RUNTIME-PERSIST MemoryContextPack, query-dependent compression, and synthesized Memory Context shall not appear in newly written .ragmem or .ragmeta.json data.
    • GHOST-MEM-REC-CODE-OWNERSHIP
      14.1. Recording Code Ownership GHOST-MEM-REC-CODE-OWNERSHIP MemoryRecord, MemoryManager, memory actions, file manager, import helpers, and file UI actions shall map to the deepest applicable UIDs in this document.
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Recording and Files Scope GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT GHOST shall record accepted input/output pairs as MemoryRecords, maintain one active memory history, persist stable bodies and current metadata through separate files, mirror metadata into SQLite, and provide controlled history-file operations.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-SYNC
    14.14. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-MEM-REC-AC-SYNC A recording baseline shall not be accepted when stable-body fields, metadata authority, file identity, capture order, SQLite mirror semantics, or history operations contradict this document.
